concepts.label: Mercury
concepts.definition: Smallest planet, closest to Sun, no atmosphere, extreme temperatures

concepts.label: Venus
concepts.definition: Second planet from Sun, hottest planet due to greenhouse effect, retrograde rotation

concepts.label: Earth

concepts.label: Mars
concepts.definition: Fourth planet, red planet, thin CO2 atmosphere, largest volcano Olympus Mons

concepts.label: Jupiter
concepts.definition: Largest planet, gas giant, Great Red Spot storm, 95 known moons

concepts.label: Saturn
concepts.definition: Sixth planet, gas giant with prominent ring system, least dense planet

concepts.label: Uranus

concepts.label: Neptune

concepts.label: Planet
concepts.definition: Celestial body orbiting a star, massive enough for gravity to make it spherical, cleared its orbit

concepts.label: Dwarf Planet

concepts.label: Solar System
concepts.definition: Gravitationally bound system of the Sun and objects orbiting it

concepts.label: Constellation
concepts.definition: Defined area of celestial sphere, 88 officially recognized by IAU

concepts.label: Zodiac

concepts.label: O-type Star
concepts.definition: Hottest main-sequence star, blue, >30000K, very massive and luminous, rare

concepts.label: B-type Star
concepts.definition: Hot blue-white star, 10000-30000K, includes many bright stars like Rigel

concepts.label: A-type Star
concepts.definition: White star, 7500-10000K, includes Sirius and Vega

concepts.label: F-type Star
concepts.definition: Yellow-white star, 6000-7500K, includes Procyon

concepts.label: G-type Main-Sequence Star
concepts.definition: Yellow star like Sun, 5200-6000K, main-sequence hydrogen fusion

concepts.label: K-type Star
concepts.definition: Orange star, 3700-5200K, includes Alpha Centauri B

concepts.label: M-type Star
concepts.definition: Red dwarf, coolest main-sequence, <3700K, most common star type

concepts.label: Red Giant
concepts.definition: Evolved star that exhausted core hydrogen, expanded and cooled, luminosity class III

concepts.label: Red Supergiant
concepts.definition: Massive evolved star, enormous radius, includes Betelgeuse

concepts.label: Blue Supergiant
concepts.definition: Massive hot luminous star, short-lived, includes Rigel

concepts.label: White Dwarf
concepts.definition: Stellar remnant of low-mass star, electron degeneracy pressure, Earth-sized

concepts.label: Neutron Star
concepts.definition: Extremely dense stellar remnant, 1.4-2 solar masses in 10km radius, from supernova

concepts.label: Pulsar
concepts.definition: Rotating neutron star emitting beams of electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Magnetar
concepts.definition: Neutron star with extremely powerful magnetic field up to 10^15 gauss

concepts.label: Black Hole
concepts.definition: Region of spacetime where gravity prevents anything including light from escaping

concepts.label: Stellar Black Hole
concepts.definition: Black hole formed from gravitational collapse of massive star, 3-100 solar masses

concepts.label: Supermassive Black Hole
concepts.definition: Black hole with mass millions to billions of solar masses at galaxy centers

concepts.label: Brown Dwarf
concepts.definition: Substellar object too low mass for hydrogen fusion, 13-80 Jupiter masses

concepts.label: T Tauri Star
concepts.definition: Young pre-main-sequence star, variable, still contracting

concepts.label: Wolf-Rayet Star
concepts.definition: Massive evolved star with strong stellar wind, losing outer hydrogen

concepts.label: Cepheid Variable
concepts.definition: Pulsating variable star with period-luminosity relation used as distance indicator

concepts.label: Binary Star
concepts.definition: Star system of two stars orbiting common center of mass

concepts.label: Eclipsing Binary
concepts.definition: Binary star system where components eclipse each other from observers perspective

concepts.label: Nova
concepts.definition: Thermonuclear explosion on white dwarf surface accreting matter from companion

concepts.label: Supernova
concepts.definition: Catastrophic stellar explosion, either core collapse or thermonuclear, extreme luminosity

concepts.label: Type Ia Supernova
concepts.definition: Thermonuclear supernova from white dwarf exceeding Chandrasekhar limit, standard candle

concepts.label: Type II Supernova
concepts.definition: Core-collapse supernova of massive star, hydrogen lines in spectrum

concepts.label: Hypernova
concepts.definition: Extremely energetic supernova, energy >10^46 joules, associated with gamma-ray bursts

concepts.label: Protostar
concepts.definition: Early stage of star formation, gravitationally collapsing cloud before nuclear fusion

concepts.label: Main Sequence
concepts.definition: Band on HR diagram where stars spend most of life fusing hydrogen to helium

concepts.label: Hertzsprung-Russell Diagram
concepts.definition: Scatter plot of stellar luminosity vs temperature showing stellar evolution stages

concepts.label: Star
concepts.definition: Luminous sphere of plasma held together by gravity undergoing nuclear fusion

concepts.label: Galaxy
concepts.definition: Gravitationally bound system of stars, gas, dust, and dark matter

concepts.label: Spiral Galaxy
concepts.definition: Galaxy with spiral arms winding from central bulge, includes Milky Way

concepts.label: Barred Spiral Galaxy
concepts.definition: Spiral galaxy with central bar-shaped structure, includes Milky Way

concepts.label: Elliptical Galaxy
concepts.definition: Galaxy with ellipsoidal shape, little gas/dust, old stars, range from dwarf to giant

concepts.label: Lenticular Galaxy
concepts.definition: Intermediate galaxy between elliptical and spiral, disk but no arms

concepts.label: Irregular Galaxy
concepts.definition: Galaxy without distinct regular shape, includes Magellanic Clouds

concepts.label: Dwarf Galaxy
concepts.definition: Small galaxy with few billion stars, most common galaxy type

concepts.label: Active Galaxy
concepts.definition: Galaxy with unusually high luminosity from active galactic nucleus

concepts.label: Seyfert Galaxy
concepts.definition: Spiral galaxy with bright active nucleus emitting broad emission lines

concepts.label: Quasar
concepts.definition: Extremely luminous active galactic nucleus powered by supermassive black hole accretion

concepts.label: Blazar
concepts.definition: Active galaxy with relativistic jet pointed toward Earth

concepts.label: Radio Galaxy
concepts.definition: Galaxy emitting large amounts of radio waves from relativistic jets

concepts.label: Starburst Galaxy
concepts.definition: Galaxy undergoing exceptionally high rate of star formation

concepts.label: Milky Way
concepts.definition: Barred spiral galaxy containing Solar System, 100-400 billion stars, 100000 ly diameter

concepts.label: Andromeda Galaxy
concepts.definition: Nearest large spiral galaxy M31, 2.5 million ly away, approaching Milky Way

concepts.label: Large Magellanic Cloud
concepts.definition: Irregular dwarf galaxy satellite of Milky Way, visible from southern hemisphere

concepts.label: Small Magellanic Cloud
concepts.definition: Dwarf galaxy satellite of Milky Way near Large Magellanic Cloud

concepts.label: Hubble Classification
concepts.definition: Galaxy morphological classification scheme: elliptical, spiral, barred spiral, irregular

concepts.label: OSIRIS-REx
concepts.definition: NASA asteroid sample return from Bennu 2023

concepts.label: Stardust
concepts.definition: Collected comet Wild 2 dust samples returned 2006

concepts.label: Deep Impact
concepts.definition: Impacted comet Tempel 1 to study interior composition 2005

concepts.label: NEAR Shoemaker
concepts.definition: First to orbit and land on asteroid Eros 2001

concepts.label: Genesis
concepts.definition: Collected solar wind samples returned to Earth 2004

concepts.label: Parker Solar Probe
concepts.definition: Closest approach to Sun, studying solar corona and wind since 2018

concepts.label: Solar Orbiter
concepts.definition: ESA solar observation mission launched 2020

concepts.label: SOHO
concepts.definition: Solar and Heliospheric Observatory at L1 since 1996

concepts.label: Ulysses
concepts.definition: First spacecraft to fly over Sun poles 1990-2009

concepts.label: Hubble Space Telescope
concepts.definition: Optical space telescope launched 1990, revolutionized astronomy

concepts.label: James Webb Space Telescope
concepts.definition: Infrared space telescope launched 2021 at L2, successor to Hubble

concepts.label: Chandra X-ray Observatory
concepts.definition: NASA X-ray space telescope launched 1999

concepts.label: Spitzer Space Telescope
concepts.definition: Infrared space telescope 2003-2020

concepts.label: Kepler Space Telescope
concepts.definition: Discovered thousands of exoplanets using transit method 2009-2018

concepts.label: TESS
concepts.definition: Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite, all-sky exoplanet survey since 2018

concepts.label: Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope
concepts.definition: Gamma-ray observatory since 2008

concepts.label: Planck
concepts.definition: ESA cosmic microwave background surveyor 2009-2013

concepts.label: WMAP
concepts.definition: Wilkinson Microwave Anisotropy Probe, CMB measurements 2001-2010

concepts.label: COBE
concepts.definition: Cosmic Background Explorer, first CMB anisotropy detection 1989-1993

concepts.label: Gaia
concepts.definition: ESA astrometry mission mapping billion stars since 2013

concepts.label: WISE
concepts.definition: Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer, all-sky infrared survey 2009

concepts.label: Europa Clipper
concepts.definition: NASA mission to study Jupiter moon Europa habitability launched 2024

concepts.label: JUICE
concepts.definition: ESA Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer launched 2023

concepts.label: Lucy
concepts.definition: NASA mission to study Jupiter Trojan asteroids launched 2021

concepts.label: DART
concepts.definition: Double Asteroid Redirection Test, first planetary defense mission, hit Dimorphos 2022

concepts.label: Artemis I
concepts.definition: Uncrewed test flight of SLS and Orion around Moon 2022

concepts.label: Artemis II
concepts.definition: First crewed Artemis mission, lunar flyby planned 2025

concepts.label: Chang'e 4
concepts.definition: First landing on far side of Moon 2019 China

concepts.label: Chang'e 5
concepts.definition: Lunar sample return mission 2020 China

concepts.label: Chandrayaan-3
concepts.definition: India successful lunar south pole landing 2023

concepts.label: SLIM
concepts.definition: JAXA Smart Lander for Investigating Moon, precision landing 2024

concepts.label: Dragonfly
concepts.definition: NASA rotorcraft mission to Titan planned launch 2028

concepts.label: Psyche
concepts.definition: NASA mission to metallic asteroid 16 Psyche launched 2023

concepts.label: VERITAS
concepts.definition: NASA Venus orbiter radar mapper planned

concepts.label: DAVINCI
concepts.definition: NASA Venus atmosphere probe planned

concepts.label: Starship
concepts.definition: SpaceX fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle, largest ever

concepts.label: Crew Dragon
concepts.definition: SpaceX crewed spacecraft for ISS missions since 2020

concepts.label: Starliner
concepts.definition: Boeing crewed spacecraft for ISS missions

concepts.label: Space Mission
concepts.definition: Organized endeavor to explore space using spacecraft

concepts.label: Up Quark
concepts.definition: Lightest quark, charge +2/3, mass ~2.2 MeV, constituent of protons and neutrons

concepts.label: Down Quark
concepts.definition: Second lightest quark, charge -1/3, mass ~4.7 MeV, constituent of protons and neutrons

concepts.label: Charm Quark
concepts.definition: Second generation quark, charge +2/3, mass ~1.27 GeV

concepts.label: Strange Quark
concepts.definition: Second generation quark, charge -1/3, mass ~95 MeV

concepts.label: Top Quark
concepts.definition: Heaviest quark and elementary particle, charge +2/3, mass ~173 GeV

concepts.label: Bottom Quark
concepts.definition: Third generation quark, charge -1/3, mass ~4.18 GeV

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: Lightest charged lepton, charge -1, mass 0.511 MeV, orbits atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Muon
concepts.definition: Second generation charged lepton, charge -1, mass 105.7 MeV, unstable

concepts.label: Tau
concepts.definition: Third generation charged lepton, charge -1, mass 1776.8 MeV, heaviest lepton

concepts.label: Electron Neutrino
concepts.definition: Neutrino associated with electron, nearly massless, weak interaction only

concepts.label: Muon Neutrino
concepts.definition: Neutrino associated with muon, nearly massless

concepts.label: Tau Neutrino
concepts.definition: Neutrino associated with tau, nearly massless, last discovered 2000

concepts.label: Photon
concepts.definition: Gauge boson mediating electromagnetic force, massless, spin 1

concepts.label: W Boson
concepts.definition: Gauge boson mediating weak force, mass ~80.4 GeV, charged W+ and W-

concepts.label: Z Boson
concepts.definition: Neutral gauge boson mediating weak force, mass ~91.2 GeV

concepts.label: Gluon
concepts.definition: Gauge boson mediating strong force, massless, carries color charge, 8 types

concepts.label: Higgs Boson
concepts.definition: Scalar boson giving mass to particles via Higgs mechanism, mass ~125 GeV, discovered 2012

concepts.label: Proton
concepts.definition: Baryon with 2 up quarks and 1 down quark, charge +1, stable

concepts.label: Neutron
concepts.definition: Baryon with 1 up quark and 2 down quarks, charge 0, half-life ~10 min free

concepts.label: Pion
concepts.definition: Lightest meson, mediates residual strong force between nucleons

concepts.label: Kaon
concepts.definition: Meson containing strange quark, important in CP violation studies

concepts.label: Antiproton
concepts.definition: Antimatter counterpart of proton, charge -1

concepts.label: Positron
concepts.definition: Antimatter counterpart of electron, charge +1, first antimatter discovered

concepts.label: Quark
concepts.definition: Elementary particle and fundamental constituent of matter, six flavors, fractional charge

concepts.label: Lepton
concepts.definition: Elementary particle not subject to strong force, includes electron and neutrinos

concepts.label: Fermion
concepts.definition: Particle with half-integer spin obeying Fermi-Dirac statistics and Pauli exclusion

concepts.label: Boson
concepts.definition: Particle with integer spin obeying Bose-Einstein statistics, force carriers

concepts.label: Baryon
concepts.definition: Composite particle of three quarks, includes protons and neutrons

concepts.label: Meson
concepts.definition: Composite particle of quark and antiquark

concepts.label: Hadron
concepts.definition: Composite particle made of quarks bound by strong force

concepts.label: Antimatter
concepts.definition: Matter composed of antiparticles with opposite charge and quantum numbers

concepts.label: Standard Model
concepts.definition: Theory describing fundamental particles and three of four forces excluding gravity

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Force
concepts.definition: Force between charged particles, infinite range, mediated by photons, relative strength 1/137

concepts.label: Strong Nuclear Force
concepts.definition: Strongest force, binds quarks in hadrons, mediated by gluons, range ~10^-15 m

concepts.label: Weak Nuclear Force
concepts.definition: Force responsible for radioactive decay, mediated by W and Z bosons, very short range

concepts.label: Gravitational Force
concepts.definition: Weakest fundamental force, infinite range, attractive between all mass-energy, described by general relativity

concepts.label: Electroweak Force
concepts.definition: Unified electromagnetic and weak forces above ~100 GeV energy scale

concepts.label: Fundamental Force
concepts.definition: One of four fundamental interactions governing all physical phenomena

concepts.label: Kinetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy of motion, KE = 1/2 mv^2 for classical objects

concepts.label: Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Stored energy due to position or configuration in a force field

concepts.label: Gravitational Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored due to position in gravitational field, PE = mgh near surface

concepts.label: Elastic Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored in deformed elastic material, PE = 1/2 kx^2

concepts.label: Chemical Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored in chemical bonds, released in reactions

concepts.label: Nuclear Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored in atomic nuclei, released in fission or fusion

concepts.label: Thermal Energy
concepts.definition: Internal energy related to temperature, kinetic energy of particles

concepts.label: Electrical Energy
concepts.definition: Energy from electric charge flow or electric fields

concepts.label: Magnetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored in magnetic field, E = B^2/2μ₀ per volume

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy carried by electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Radiant Energy
concepts.definition: Energy of electromagnetic waves including light

concepts.label: Sound Energy
concepts.definition: Energy carried by sound waves through mechanical vibrations

concepts.label: Mechanical Energy
concepts.definition: Sum of kinetic and potential energy in a system

concepts.label: Dark Energy
concepts.definition: Hypothetical energy causing accelerating expansion of universe, ~68% of universe

concepts.label: Vacuum Energy
concepts.definition: Energy of empty space from quantum field fluctuations

concepts.label: Rest Energy
concepts.definition: Energy equivalent of rest mass, E = mc^2

concepts.label: Binding Energy
concepts.definition: Energy required to disassemble system into separate parts

concepts.label: Ionization Energy
concepts.definition: Energy required to remove electron from atom or molecule

concepts.label: Energy
concepts.definition: Quantitative property transferred to or from a body to perform work or heat, conserved in isolated systems

concepts.label: Newton's First Law
concepts.definition: Object at rest stays at rest, object in motion stays in uniform motion unless acted on by net force

concepts.label: Newton's Second Law
concepts.definition: Net force equals mass times acceleration F=ma, fundamental equation of classical mechanics

concepts.label: Newton's Third Law
concepts.definition: Every action has equal and opposite reaction, forces always occur in pairs

concepts.label: Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation
concepts.definition: Gravitational force between two masses F = GMm/r^2

concepts.label: Kepler's First Law
concepts.definition: Planets orbit in ellipses with Sun at one focus

concepts.label: Kepler's Second Law
concepts.definition: Line from Sun to planet sweeps equal areas in equal times

concepts.label: Kepler's Third Law
concepts.definition: Square of orbital period proportional to cube of semi-major axis T^2 ∝ a^3

concepts.label: Conservation of Energy
concepts.definition: Total energy in isolated system remains constant, energy cannot be created or destroyed

concepts.label: Conservation of Momentum
concepts.definition: Total momentum of isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Conservation of Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: Total angular momentum remains constant when no external torque

concepts.label: Conservation of Charge
concepts.definition: Total electric charge in isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Work-Energy Theorem
concepts.definition: Net work done on object equals change in kinetic energy W = ΔKE

concepts.label: Impulse-Momentum Theorem
concepts.definition: Impulse equals change in momentum FΔt = Δp

concepts.label: Hooke's Law
concepts.definition: Force of spring proportional to displacement F = -kx for small deformations

concepts.label: Archimedes' Principle
concepts.definition: Buoyant force equals weight of displaced fluid

concepts.label: Pascal's Law
concepts.definition: Pressure change applied to enclosed fluid transmitted equally throughout

concepts.label: Bernoulli's Principle
concepts.definition: Increase in fluid speed occurs with decrease in pressure or potential energy

concepts.label: Torricelli's Theorem
concepts.definition: Speed of fluid flowing from orifice v = sqrt(2gh)

concepts.label: Continuity Equation
concepts.definition: Mass flow rate constant in steady flow A₁v₁ = A₂v₂ for incompressible fluid

concepts.label: Navier-Stokes Equations
concepts.definition: Equations of motion for viscous fluid flow, millennium prize problem

concepts.label: Euler's Equations of Motion
concepts.definition: Equations for inviscid fluid flow

concepts.label: D'Alembert's Principle
concepts.definition: Sum of forces minus inertial forces equals zero for dynamic systems

concepts.label: Hamilton's Principle
concepts.definition: Actual path minimizes action integral, foundation of Lagrangian mechanics

concepts.label: Lagrangian Mechanics
concepts.definition: Reformulation of mechanics using generalized coordinates and Lagrangian L=T-V

concepts.label: Hamiltonian Mechanics
concepts.definition: Reformulation of mechanics using Hamilton's equations with phase space

concepts.label: Noether's Theorem
concepts.definition: Every continuous symmetry of action corresponds to conservation law

concepts.label: Virial Theorem
concepts.definition: Average kinetic energy related to average potential energy for bound systems

concepts.label: Principle of Least Action
concepts.definition: Physical system follows path that minimizes action integral

concepts.label: Galilean Relativity
concepts.definition: Laws of physics same in all inertial frames, Galilean transformations

concepts.label: General Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory: gravity is curvature of spacetime caused by mass-energy

concepts.label: Special Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory: speed of light constant in all frames, E=mc^2, time dilation

concepts.label: Mass-Energy Equivalence
concepts.definition: E = mc^2, energy and mass are interchangeable

concepts.label: Time Dilation
concepts.definition: Moving clocks run slower, t = γt₀ where γ = 1/√(1-v²/c²)

concepts.label: Length Contraction
concepts.definition: Moving objects shortened in direction of motion L = L₀/γ

concepts.label: Lorentz Transformation
concepts.definition: Coordinate transformations between inertial frames in special relativity

concepts.label: Gravitational Time Dilation
concepts.definition: Clocks run slower in stronger gravitational fields

concepts.label: Gravitational Lensing
concepts.definition: Light bent by massive objects curving spacetime

concepts.label: Gravitational Waves
concepts.definition: Ripples in spacetime from accelerating masses, detected 2015 by LIGO

concepts.label: Einstein Field Equations
concepts.definition: Gμν + Λgμν = 8πG/c⁴ Tμν, relate spacetime geometry to energy-momentum

concepts.label: Schwarzschild Metric
concepts.definition: Solution to Einstein equations for spherically symmetric non-rotating mass, defines black hole

concepts.label: Kerr Metric
concepts.definition: Solution for rotating black hole spacetime

concepts.label: Equivalence Principle
concepts.definition: Gravitational and inertial mass are equivalent, foundation of general relativity

concepts.label: Frame Dragging
concepts.definition: Rotating massive object drags spacetime around it, Lense-Thirring effect

concepts.label: Geodesic Equation
concepts.definition: Equation of motion for free particles in curved spacetime

concepts.label: Friedmann Equations
concepts.definition: Govern expansion of space in homogeneous isotropic universe

concepts.label: Cosmological Constant
concepts.definition: Lambda term in Einstein equations, represents dark energy density

concepts.label: Coulomb's Law
concepts.definition: Electric force between charges F = kq₁q₂/r^2

concepts.label: Gauss's Law
concepts.definition: Electric flux through closed surface proportional to enclosed charge

concepts.label: Gauss's Law for Magnetism
concepts.definition: Magnetic flux through any closed surface is zero, no magnetic monopoles

concepts.label: Faraday's Law of Induction
concepts.definition: Changing magnetic flux induces electromotive force EMF = -dΦ/dt

concepts.label: Ampere's Law
concepts.definition: Magnetic field around current proportional to current, with Maxwell correction

concepts.label: Maxwell's Equations
concepts.definition: Four equations unifying electricity and magnetism, predict electromagnetic waves

concepts.label: Biot-Savart Law
concepts.definition: Magnetic field from current element dB = μ₀/4π (Idl×r̂)/r²

concepts.label: Lorentz Force Law
concepts.definition: Force on charge in EM field F = q(E + v×B)

concepts.label: Ohm's Law
concepts.definition: Voltage equals current times resistance V = IR

concepts.label: Kirchhoff's Current Law
concepts.definition: Sum of currents entering junction equals sum leaving, charge conservation

concepts.label: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law
concepts.definition: Sum of voltage drops around closed loop equals zero, energy conservation

concepts.label: Joule's Law
concepts.definition: Power dissipated by resistor P = I²R

concepts.label: Lenz's Law
concepts.definition: Induced current opposes change in magnetic flux that produced it

concepts.label: Poynting Vector
concepts.definition: S = E×B/μ₀, energy flux of electromagnetic field

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Induction
concepts.definition: Production of EMF by changing magnetic field through conductor

concepts.label: Skin Effect
concepts.definition: Alternating current concentrates near conductor surface at high frequencies

concepts.label: Displacement Current
concepts.definition: Maxwell's addition to Ampere's law, changing electric field acts as current

concepts.label: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: If A in thermal equilibrium with B and B with C then A with C, defines temperature

concepts.label: First Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Energy conservation: change in internal energy equals heat added minus work done ΔU=Q-W

concepts.label: Second Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy of isolated system never decreases, heat flows from hot to cold spontaneously

concepts.label: Third Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy approaches constant as temperature approaches absolute zero

concepts.label: Ideal Gas Law
concepts.definition: PV = nRT relating pressure volume temperature and amount of ideal gas

concepts.label: Boyle's Law
concepts.definition: At constant temperature gas pressure inversely proportional to volume P₁V₁=P₂V₂

concepts.label: Charles's Law
concepts.definition: At constant pressure gas volume proportional to temperature V/T=constant

concepts.label: Gay-Lussac's Law
concepts.definition: At constant volume gas pressure proportional to temperature P/T=constant

concepts.label: Avogadro's Law
concepts.definition: Equal volumes of gases at same T and P contain equal number of molecules

concepts.label: Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures
concepts.definition: Total pressure of gas mixture equals sum of partial pressures

concepts.label: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
concepts.definition: Total radiated power proportional to T^4, P = σAT^4

concepts.label: Wien's Displacement Law
concepts.definition: Peak wavelength of blackbody inversely proportional to temperature λmax=b/T

concepts.label: Planck's Law
concepts.definition: Spectral radiance of blackbody as function of wavelength and temperature

concepts.label: Boltzmann Distribution
concepts.definition: Probability of state proportional to e^(-E/kT), statistical mechanics foundation

concepts.label: Maxwell-Boltzmann Distribution
concepts.definition: Speed distribution of particles in ideal gas at thermal equilibrium

concepts.label: Carnot's Theorem
concepts.definition: No heat engine more efficient than Carnot engine between same temperatures

concepts.label: Clausius Inequality
concepts.definition: Cyclic integral of δQ/T ≤ 0, equality for reversible processes

concepts.label: Fourier's Law of Heat Conduction
concepts.definition: Heat flux proportional to negative temperature gradient q = -k∇T

concepts.label: Newton's Law of Cooling
concepts.definition: Rate of heat loss proportional to temperature difference with surroundings

concepts.label: Equipartition Theorem
concepts.definition: Each quadratic degree of freedom contributes 1/2 kT to average energy

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: Measure of disorder or number of microstates, S = k ln Ω in statistical mechanics

concepts.label: Schrödinger Equation
concepts.definition: Fundamental equation of quantum mechanics describing wave function evolution iℏ∂ψ/∂t = Ĥψ

concepts.label: Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
concepts.definition: Cannot simultaneously know exact position and momentum ΔxΔp ≥ ℏ/2

concepts.label: Pauli Exclusion Principle
concepts.definition: No two identical fermions can occupy same quantum state simultaneously

concepts.label: Wave-Particle Duality
concepts.definition: All matter exhibits both wave and particle properties

concepts.label: De Broglie Hypothesis
concepts.definition: Every particle has associated wavelength λ = h/p

concepts.label: Photoelectric Effect
concepts.definition: Light ejects electrons from metal, energy depends on frequency not intensity

concepts.label: Compton Scattering
concepts.definition: X-ray photon wavelength increases when scattered by electron

concepts.label: Born Rule
concepts.definition: Probability of measurement outcome is square of wave function amplitude |ψ|²

concepts.label: Superposition Principle (QM)
concepts.definition: Quantum system can exist in multiple states simultaneously until measured

concepts.label: Quantum Tunneling
concepts.definition: Particle passing through potential barrier higher than its energy

concepts.label: Quantum Entanglement
concepts.definition: Correlated quantum states where measuring one instantly determines the other

concepts.label: Bell's Theorem
concepts.definition: No local hidden variable theory can reproduce all quantum mechanical predictions

concepts.label: Copenhagen Interpretation
concepts.definition: Wave function collapses upon measurement, no definite state before observation

concepts.label: Many-Worlds Interpretation
concepts.definition: All possible quantum measurement outcomes are realized in branching universes

concepts.label: Dirac Equation
concepts.definition: Relativistic quantum equation for spin-1/2 particles, predicted antimatter

concepts.label: Klein-Gordon Equation
concepts.definition: Relativistic quantum equation for spin-0 particles

concepts.label: Spin
concepts.definition: Intrinsic angular momentum of elementary particles, quantized in half-integer or integer units

concepts.label: Quantum Field Theory
concepts.definition: Framework combining quantum mechanics and special relativity, particles as field excitations

concepts.label: Quantum Electrodynamics
concepts.definition: QFT of electromagnetic interaction, most precisely tested theory in physics

concepts.label: Quantum Chromodynamics
concepts.definition: QFT of strong interaction between quarks and gluons

concepts.label: Renormalization
concepts.definition: Technique to handle infinities in quantum field theory calculations

concepts.label: Feynman Diagrams
concepts.definition: Pictorial representation of particle interactions in quantum field theory

concepts.label: Path Integral Formulation
concepts.definition: Feynman's formulation summing over all possible paths

concepts.label: Bose-Einstein Condensate
concepts.definition: State of matter at near absolute zero where bosons occupy same quantum state

concepts.label: Fermi-Dirac Statistics
concepts.definition: Statistical mechanics for identical fermions obeying exclusion principle

concepts.label: Bose-Einstein Statistics
concepts.definition: Statistical mechanics for identical bosons that can share quantum states

concepts.label: Casimir Effect
concepts.definition: Attractive force between close parallel plates from quantum vacuum fluctuations

concepts.label: Lamb Shift
concepts.definition: Small energy difference in hydrogen levels from quantum fluctuations, confirmed QED

concepts.label: Stern-Gerlach Experiment
concepts.definition: Demonstrated quantization of angular momentum with silver atoms in magnetic field

concepts.label: Double-Slit Experiment
concepts.definition: Demonstrates wave-particle duality, single particles create interference pattern

concepts.label: Quantum Decoherence
concepts.definition: Loss of quantum coherence through interaction with environment

concepts.label: No-Cloning Theorem
concepts.definition: Impossible to create identical copy of arbitrary unknown quantum state

concepts.label: Correspondence Principle
concepts.definition: Quantum mechanics reproduces classical physics in limit of large quantum numbers

concepts.label: Nuclear Fission
concepts.definition: Splitting heavy atomic nucleus into lighter nuclei releasing energy

concepts.label: Nuclear Fusion
concepts.definition: Combining light atomic nuclei into heavier nucleus releasing energy

concepts.label: Radioactive Decay
concepts.definition: Spontaneous emission of radiation from unstable atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Alpha Decay
concepts.definition: Nucleus emits alpha particle (helium-4 nucleus), reduces atomic number by 2

concepts.label: Beta Decay
concepts.definition: Neutron converts to proton emitting electron and antineutrino, or vice versa

concepts.label: Gamma Decay
concepts.definition: Excited nucleus emits gamma ray photon, no change in atomic number

concepts.label: Half-Life
concepts.definition: Time for half of radioactive sample to decay, characteristic of each isotope

concepts.label: Binding Energy per Nucleon
concepts.definition: Average energy needed to remove nucleon from nucleus, peaks at iron-56

concepts.label: Strong Nuclear Force (Residual)
concepts.definition: Force binding protons and neutrons in nucleus, residual of color force

concepts.label: Nuclear Shell Model
concepts.definition: Model of nucleus with nucleons in quantum energy levels like electron shells

concepts.label: Liquid Drop Model
concepts.definition: Nuclear model treating nucleus as incompressible fluid drop

concepts.label: Bethe-Weizsäcker Formula
concepts.definition: Semi-empirical mass formula for nuclear binding energy

concepts.label: Cross Section
concepts.definition: Effective area for nuclear or particle interaction, measured in barns

concepts.label: Bohr Model
concepts.definition: Atomic model with quantized electron orbits and energy levels E_n = -13.6/n² eV for hydrogen

concepts.label: Rydberg Formula
concepts.definition: Predicts wavelengths of hydrogen spectral lines 1/λ = R(1/n₁² - 1/n₂²)

concepts.label: Atomic Orbital
concepts.definition: Wave function describing probability of finding electron around nucleus

concepts.label: Quantum Numbers
concepts.definition: Set of four numbers (n, l, ml, ms) describing electron state in atom

concepts.label: Aufbau Principle
concepts.definition: Electrons fill lowest energy orbitals first

concepts.label: Hund's Rule
concepts.definition: Electrons fill degenerate orbitals singly before pairing with parallel spins

concepts.label: Zeeman Effect
concepts.definition: Splitting of spectral lines in magnetic field

concepts.label: Stark Effect
concepts.definition: Splitting of spectral lines in electric field

concepts.label: Fine Structure
concepts.definition: Splitting of spectral lines from spin-orbit coupling and relativistic effects

concepts.label: Hyperfine Structure
concepts.definition: Small energy splitting from nuclear spin interaction, basis of atomic clocks

concepts.label: Snell's Law
concepts.definition: n₁sinθ₁ = n₂sinθ₂, relates angles of incidence and refraction at interface

concepts.label: Law of Reflection
concepts.definition: Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection

concepts.label: Total Internal Reflection
concepts.definition: Light completely reflected at interface when angle exceeds critical angle

concepts.label: Brewster's Angle
concepts.definition: Angle at which reflected light is completely polarized tan θ_B = n₂/n₁

concepts.label: Diffraction
concepts.definition: Bending of waves around obstacles or through apertures

concepts.label: Interference
concepts.definition: Superposition of waves producing reinforcement or cancellation

concepts.label: Constructive Interference
concepts.definition: Waves in phase combine to produce larger amplitude

concepts.label: Destructive Interference
concepts.definition: Waves out of phase cancel each other

concepts.label: Thin Film Interference
concepts.definition: Interference from reflections at top and bottom of thin film

concepts.label: Huygens' Principle
concepts.definition: Every point on wavefront is source of secondary spherical wavelets

concepts.label: Fresnel Equations
concepts.definition: Describe reflection and transmission of light at interface for each polarization

concepts.label: Rayleigh Scattering
concepts.definition: Scattering of light by particles smaller than wavelength, explains blue sky

concepts.label: Mie Scattering
concepts.definition: Scattering by particles comparable to wavelength, explains white clouds

concepts.label: Raman Scattering
concepts.definition: Inelastic scattering where photon energy shifts by vibrational mode

concepts.label: Polarization
concepts.definition: Orientation of oscillation plane of transverse wave like light

concepts.label: Malus's Law
concepts.definition: Intensity through polarizer I = I₀cos²θ

concepts.label: Diffraction Grating
concepts.definition: Optical component with periodic structure dispersing light into spectra

concepts.label: Airy Disk
concepts.definition: Central bright spot of diffraction pattern from circular aperture

concepts.label: Rayleigh Criterion
concepts.definition: Minimum angular separation for resolving two point sources θ = 1.22λ/D

concepts.label: Optical Fiber
concepts.definition: Flexible transparent fiber guiding light by total internal reflection

concepts.label: Laser
concepts.definition: Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation, coherent monochromatic light

concepts.label: Stimulated Emission
concepts.definition: Photon triggers emission of identical photon from excited atom

concepts.label: Spontaneous Emission
concepts.definition: Excited atom emits photon randomly without external trigger

concepts.label: Absorption Spectrum
concepts.definition: Dark lines in continuous spectrum from absorption by cooler gas

concepts.label: Emission Spectrum
concepts.definition: Bright lines at specific wavelengths from excited atoms

concepts.label: Blackbody Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation emitted by perfect absorber in thermal equilibrium

concepts.label: Cherenkov Radiation
concepts.definition: Light emitted when charged particle moves faster than light in medium

concepts.label: Synchrotron Radiation
concepts.definition: Radiation from charged particles accelerating in magnetic field

concepts.label: Bremsstrahlung
concepts.definition: Radiation from deceleration of charged particle by another, braking radiation

concepts.label: Wave Equation
concepts.definition: ∂²u/∂t² = c²∂²u/∂x², describes propagation of waves

concepts.label: Doppler Effect
concepts.definition: Change in observed frequency when source and observer in relative motion

concepts.label: Redshift
concepts.definition: Increase in wavelength of light from receding source or expanding space

concepts.label: Blueshift
concepts.definition: Decrease in wavelength of light from approaching source

concepts.label: Hubble's Law
concepts.definition: Recession velocity of galaxy proportional to distance v = H₀d

concepts.label: Standing Wave
concepts.definition: Wave pattern from superposition of two traveling waves in opposite directions

concepts.label: Resonance
concepts.definition: Large amplitude oscillation when driving frequency matches natural frequency

concepts.label: Harmonics
concepts.definition: Integer multiples of fundamental frequency in standing wave

concepts.label: Fourier Analysis
concepts.definition: Decomposition of function into sum of sinusoidal components

concepts.label: Superposition Principle (Waves)
concepts.definition: Net displacement is sum of individual wave displacements at each point

concepts.label: Dispersion
concepts.definition: Dependence of wave speed on frequency, causes spreading of wave packet

concepts.label: Group Velocity
concepts.definition: Velocity of wave packet envelope, speed of energy/information transport

concepts.label: Phase Velocity
concepts.definition: Speed of individual wave crest, v_p = ω/k

concepts.label: Speed of Sound
concepts.definition: ~343 m/s in air at 20°C, depends on medium properties

concepts.label: Decibel
concepts.definition: Logarithmic unit of sound intensity, dB = 10 log(I/I₀)

concepts.label: Acoustic Resonance
concepts.definition: Reinforcement of sound in cavity at natural frequencies

concepts.label: Doppler Effect (Sound)
concepts.definition: Frequency shift of sound from relative motion of source and observer

concepts.label: Sonic Boom
concepts.definition: Shock wave produced when object travels faster than speed of sound

concepts.label: Mach Number
concepts.definition: Ratio of object speed to speed of sound, Mach 1 = speed of sound

concepts.label: Ultrasound
concepts.definition: Sound waves above human hearing range >20 kHz, used in medical imaging

concepts.label: Infrasound
concepts.definition: Sound waves below human hearing range <20 Hz

concepts.label: Big Bang Theory
concepts.definition: Universe began from extremely hot dense state ~13.8 billion years ago and has been expanding

concepts.label: Cosmic Microwave Background
concepts.definition: Thermal radiation from early universe ~380000 years after Big Bang, 2.725 K

concepts.label: Cosmic Inflation
concepts.definition: Exponential expansion of early universe in first ~10^-36 seconds

concepts.label: Dark Matter
concepts.definition: Invisible matter making up ~27% of universe, detectable only through gravity

concepts.label: Dark Energy
concepts.definition: Mysterious energy causing accelerating expansion of universe, ~68% of universe

concepts.label: Hubble Constant
concepts.definition: Rate of expansion of universe, ~70 km/s/Mpc, tension between measurement methods

concepts.label: Olbers' Paradox
concepts.definition: Why is night sky dark if universe has infinite stars, resolved by finite age and expansion

concepts.label: Cosmic Distance Measurement
concepts.definition: Methods to determine astronomical distances: parallax, Cepheids, Type Ia supernovae

concepts.label: Parallax
concepts.definition: Apparent shift in position of nearby star due to Earth orbital motion, distance measurement

concepts.label: Parsec
concepts.definition: Distance at which 1 AU subtends 1 arcsecond parallax, ~3.26 light-years

concepts.label: Light-Year
concepts.definition: Distance light travels in one year, ~9.461 × 10^12 km

concepts.label: Astronomical Unit
concepts.definition: Mean Earth-Sun distance, ~149.6 million km

concepts.label: Cosmological Principle
concepts.definition: Universe is homogeneous and isotropic on large scales

concepts.label: Nucleosynthesis
concepts.definition: Formation of atomic nuclei, Big Bang produced H He Li, stars produce heavier elements

concepts.label: Stellar Nucleosynthesis
concepts.definition: Creation of elements in stellar cores through nuclear fusion

concepts.label: Chandrasekhar Limit
concepts.definition: Maximum mass of stable white dwarf ~1.4 solar masses

concepts.label: Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff Limit
concepts.definition: Maximum mass of neutron star ~2-3 solar masses

concepts.label: Eddington Limit
concepts.definition: Maximum luminosity where radiation pressure balances gravity

concepts.label: Roche Limit
concepts.definition: Minimum distance satellite can approach primary without being torn apart by tides

concepts.label: Schwarzschild Radius
concepts.definition: Radius of event horizon for non-rotating black hole r_s = 2GM/c²

concepts.label: Event Horizon
concepts.definition: Boundary around black hole beyond which nothing can escape

concepts.label: Hawking Radiation
concepts.definition: Theoretical radiation from black holes due to quantum effects near event horizon

concepts.label: Information Paradox
concepts.definition: Conflict between quantum mechanics unitarity and black hole information loss

concepts.label: Penrose Process
concepts.definition: Extraction of energy from rotating black hole ergosphere

concepts.label: Cosmic Web
concepts.definition: Large-scale structure of universe: filaments, walls, voids of galaxy clusters

concepts.label: Great Attractor
concepts.definition: Gravitational anomaly in intergalactic space attracting local galaxy flow

concepts.label: Multiverse
concepts.definition: Hypothetical set of multiple universes comprising all of physical reality

concepts.label: String Theory
concepts.definition: Theoretical framework where point particles replaced by one-dimensional strings

concepts.label: M-Theory
concepts.definition: Unifying framework of five superstring theories in 11 dimensions

concepts.label: Loop Quantum Gravity
concepts.definition: Theory quantizing spacetime itself into discrete units

concepts.label: Grand Unified Theory
concepts.definition: Hypothetical theory unifying strong, weak, and electromagnetic forces

concepts.label: Theory of Everything
concepts.definition: Hypothetical single framework explaining all fundamental forces and particles

concepts.label: Anthropic Principle
concepts.definition: Universe parameters appear fine-tuned for conscious life observation

concepts.label: Fermi Paradox
concepts.definition: Apparent contradiction between lack of evidence for extraterrestrial civilizations and high probability

concepts.label: Drake Equation
concepts.definition: Probabilistic formula estimating number of communicating civilizations in galaxy

concepts.label: Centripetal Force
concepts.definition: Force directed toward center of circular path, F = mv²/r

concepts.label: Centrifugal Force
concepts.definition: Pseudo-force in rotating frame pointing outward from rotation axis

concepts.label: Coriolis Force
concepts.definition: Pseudo-force in rotating frame deflecting moving objects perpendicular to velocity

concepts.label: Friction
concepts.definition: Force opposing relative motion between surfaces in contact

concepts.label: Drag Force
concepts.definition: Resistance force on object moving through fluid, F_d = 1/2 ρv²C_dA

concepts.label: Terminal Velocity
concepts.definition: Maximum velocity of falling object when drag equals gravity

concepts.label: Simple Harmonic Motion
concepts.definition: Oscillation with restoring force proportional to displacement, sinusoidal motion

concepts.label: Damped Oscillation
concepts.definition: Oscillation with decreasing amplitude due to energy loss

concepts.label: Forced Oscillation
concepts.definition: Oscillation driven by external periodic force

concepts.label: Pendulum
concepts.definition: Weight suspended from pivot swinging freely, period T = 2π√(L/g) for small angles

concepts.label: Moment of Inertia
concepts.definition: Rotational analog of mass, resistance to angular acceleration I = Σmr²

concepts.label: Torque
concepts.definition: Rotational force, τ = r × F, causes angular acceleration

concepts.label: Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: Rotational analog of linear momentum L = Iω or L = r × p

concepts.label: Precession
concepts.definition: Change in orientation of rotational axis of spinning body due to torque

concepts.label: Gyroscope
concepts.definition: Device using spinning rotor to maintain orientation, precession effects

concepts.label: Elastic Collision
concepts.definition: Collision conserving both kinetic energy and momentum

concepts.label: Inelastic Collision
concepts.definition: Collision conserving momentum but not kinetic energy

concepts.label: Center of Mass
concepts.definition: Point where weighted mass distribution is centered, system moves as if all mass concentrated there

concepts.label: Rigid Body Dynamics
concepts.definition: Mechanics of solid bodies that do not deform

concepts.label: Electric Field
concepts.definition: Region where electric charge experiences force, E = F/q

concepts.label: Magnetic Field
concepts.definition: Region where moving charges or magnets experience force

concepts.label: Electric Potential
concepts.definition: Energy per unit charge at point in electric field, voltage V = W/q

concepts.label: Capacitance
concepts.definition: Ability to store electric charge C = Q/V, unit farad

concepts.label: Inductance
concepts.definition: Property opposing change in current, stores energy in magnetic field, unit henry

concepts.label: Resistance
concepts.definition: Opposition to current flow, R = V/I, unit ohm

concepts.label: Impedance
concepts.definition: Total opposition to AC current including resistance and reactance Z = R + jX

concepts.label: RC Circuit
concepts.definition: Circuit with resistor and capacitor, time constant τ = RC

concepts.label: RL Circuit
concepts.definition: Circuit with resistor and inductor, time constant τ = L/R

concepts.label: RLC Circuit
concepts.definition: Circuit with resistor inductor capacitor, exhibits resonance

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Wave
concepts.definition: Self-propagating wave of oscillating electric and magnetic fields, speed c in vacuum

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Spectrum
concepts.definition: Range of all EM radiation frequencies from radio to gamma rays

concepts.label: Radio Waves
concepts.definition: EM radiation with longest wavelength >1mm, used in communication

concepts.label: Microwaves
concepts.definition: EM radiation 1mm to 1m wavelength, used in radar and cooking

concepts.label: Infrared Radiation
concepts.definition: EM radiation between visible light and microwaves, thermal radiation

concepts.label: Visible Light
concepts.definition: EM radiation visible to human eye, wavelength 380-700 nm

concepts.label: Ultraviolet Radiation
concepts.definition: EM radiation shorter than visible light, causes sunburn

concepts.label: X-rays
concepts.definition: High-energy EM radiation, penetrates soft tissue, used in medical imaging

concepts.label: Gamma Rays
concepts.definition: Highest energy EM radiation from nuclear decay and cosmic sources

concepts.label: Semiconductor
concepts.definition: Material with conductivity between conductor and insulator, basis of electronics

concepts.label: Superconductivity
concepts.definition: Zero electrical resistance below critical temperature, Meissner effect

concepts.label: Hall Effect
concepts.definition: Voltage across conductor perpendicular to current and magnetic field

concepts.label: Piezoelectric Effect
concepts.definition: Generation of electric charge from mechanical stress in certain crystals

concepts.label: Thermoelectric Effect
concepts.definition: Direct conversion between temperature difference and electric voltage

concepts.label: Photoelectric Effect Law
concepts.definition: KEmax = hf - φ, Einstein's equation for photoelectric effect

concepts.label: Band Gap
concepts.definition: Energy range in solid where no electron states exist, determines electrical properties

concepts.label: Fermi Energy
concepts.definition: Highest energy level occupied by electrons at absolute zero

concepts.label: Meter
concepts.definition: SI base unit of length, defined by speed of light

concepts.label: Kilogram
concepts.definition: SI base unit of mass, defined by Planck constant since 2019

concepts.label: Second
concepts.definition: SI base unit of time, defined by cesium-133 atom transitions

concepts.label: Ampere
concepts.definition: SI base unit of electric current, defined by elementary charge

concepts.label: Kelvin
concepts.definition: SI base unit of temperature, defined by Boltzmann constant

concepts.label: Mole
concepts.definition: SI base unit of amount of substance, Avogadro number of entities

concepts.label: Candela
concepts.definition: SI base unit of luminous intensity

concepts.label: Newton
concepts.definition: SI unit of force, 1 N = 1 kg⋅m/s²

concepts.label: Joule
concepts.definition: SI unit of energy, 1 J = 1 N⋅m = 1 kg⋅m²/s²

concepts.label: Watt
concepts.definition: SI unit of power, 1 W = 1 J/s

concepts.label: Pascal
concepts.definition: SI unit of pressure, 1 Pa = 1 N/m²

concepts.label: Hertz
concepts.definition: SI unit of frequency, 1 Hz = 1 cycle per second

concepts.label: Coulomb
concepts.definition: SI unit of electric charge, 1 C = 1 A⋅s

concepts.label: Volt
concepts.definition: SI unit of electric potential, 1 V = 1 J/C

concepts.label: Ohm
concepts.definition: SI unit of electrical resistance, 1 Ω = 1 V/A

concepts.label: Farad
concepts.definition: SI unit of capacitance, 1 F = 1 C/V

concepts.label: Henry
concepts.definition: SI unit of inductance, 1 H = 1 V⋅s/A

concepts.label: Weber
concepts.definition: SI unit of magnetic flux, 1 Wb = 1 V⋅s

concepts.label: Tesla
concepts.definition: SI unit of magnetic flux density, 1 T = 1 Wb/m²

concepts.label: Siemens
concepts.definition: SI unit of electrical conductance, 1 S = 1/Ω

concepts.label: Lumen
concepts.definition: SI unit of luminous flux

concepts.label: Lux
concepts.definition: SI unit of illuminance, 1 lx = 1 lm/m²

concepts.label: Becquerel
concepts.definition: SI unit of radioactivity, 1 Bq = 1 decay per second

concepts.label: Gray
concepts.definition: SI unit of absorbed radiation dose, 1 Gy = 1 J/kg

concepts.label: Sievert
concepts.definition: SI unit of equivalent radiation dose, accounts for biological effect

concepts.label: Radian
concepts.definition: SI unit of plane angle, full circle = 2π radians

concepts.label: Steradian
concepts.definition: SI unit of solid angle

concepts.label: Katal
concepts.definition: SI unit of catalytic activity

concepts.label: Electronvolt
concepts.definition: Energy unit in particle physics, 1 eV = 1.602×10⁻¹⁹ J

concepts.label: Angstrom
concepts.definition: Unit of length, 1 Å = 10⁻¹⁰ m, used for atomic scales

concepts.label: Bar
concepts.definition: Unit of pressure, 1 bar = 100000 Pa

concepts.label: Atmosphere
concepts.definition: Unit of pressure, 1 atm = 101325 Pa, standard atmospheric pressure

concepts.label: Calorie
concepts.definition: Unit of energy, 1 cal = 4.184 J, energy to heat 1g water by 1°C

concepts.label: Kilowatt-hour
concepts.definition: Unit of energy, 1 kWh = 3.6 MJ, common for electricity billing

concepts.label: Horsepower
concepts.definition: Unit of power, 1 hp ≈ 746 W

concepts.label: Pound-force
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of force, 1 lbf ≈ 4.448 N

concepts.label: Mile
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of length, 1 mi = 1609.344 m

concepts.label: Foot
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of length, 1 ft = 0.3048 m

concepts.label: Inch
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of length, 1 in = 25.4 mm

concepts.label: Yard
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of length, 1 yd = 0.9144 m

concepts.label: Pound Mass
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of mass, 1 lb = 0.4536 kg

concepts.label: Ounce
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of mass, 1 oz = 28.35 g

concepts.label: Gallon
concepts.definition: Imperial unit of volume, US gallon = 3.785 L

concepts.label: Liter
concepts.definition: Metric unit of volume, 1 L = 0.001 m³

concepts.label: Nautical Mile
concepts.definition: Unit of distance, 1 nmi = 1852 m, one minute of latitude

concepts.label: Knot
concepts.definition: Unit of speed, 1 knot = 1 nautical mile per hour

concepts.label: Solar Mass
concepts.definition: Unit of mass in astronomy, mass of Sun ~1.989×10³⁰ kg

concepts.label: Solar Luminosity
concepts.definition: Unit of luminosity, luminosity of Sun ~3.828×10²⁶ W

concepts.label: Solar Radius
concepts.definition: Unit of length in astronomy, radius of Sun ~6.957×10⁸ m

concepts.label: Jupiter Mass
concepts.definition: Unit of mass, ~1.898×10²⁷ kg, used for exoplanets

concepts.label: Earth Mass
concepts.definition: Unit of mass, ~5.972×10²⁴ kg

concepts.label: Planck Length
concepts.definition: Smallest meaningful length ~1.616×10⁻³⁵ m

concepts.label: Planck Time
concepts.definition: Smallest meaningful time interval ~5.391×10⁻⁴⁴ s

concepts.label: Planck Mass
concepts.definition: Natural unit of mass ~2.176×10⁻⁸ kg

concepts.label: Planck Temperature
concepts.definition: Natural unit of temperature ~1.416×10³² K

concepts.label: Planck Energy
concepts.definition: Natural unit of energy ~1.956×10⁹ J

concepts.label: Barn
concepts.definition: Unit of area in nuclear physics, 1 b = 10⁻²⁸ m²

concepts.label: Curie
concepts.definition: Old unit of radioactivity, 1 Ci = 3.7×10¹⁰ Bq

concepts.label: Roentgen
concepts.definition: Old unit of radiation exposure

concepts.label: Gauss
concepts.definition: CGS unit of magnetic flux density, 1 G = 10⁻⁴ T

concepts.label: Dyne
concepts.definition: CGS unit of force, 1 dyn = 10⁻⁵ N

concepts.label: Erg
concepts.definition: CGS unit of energy, 1 erg = 10⁻⁷ J

concepts.label: Poise
concepts.definition: CGS unit of dynamic viscosity

concepts.label: Stokes
concepts.definition: CGS unit of kinematic viscosity

concepts.label: Jansky
concepts.definition: Unit of spectral flux density in radio astronomy, 1 Jy = 10⁻²⁶ W/m²/Hz

concepts.label: Magnitude (Apparent)
concepts.definition: Logarithmic measure of brightness of celestial object as seen from Earth

concepts.label: Magnitude (Absolute)
concepts.definition: Brightness of celestial object at standard distance of 10 parsecs

concepts.label: Celsius
concepts.definition: Temperature scale, 0°C = water freezing, 100°C = boiling at 1 atm

concepts.label: Fahrenheit
concepts.definition: Temperature scale, 32°F = water freezing, 212°F = boiling at 1 atm

concepts.label: Rankine
concepts.definition: Absolute temperature scale based on Fahrenheit degrees

concepts.label: Minute
concepts.definition: Unit of time, 60 seconds

concepts.label: Hour
concepts.definition: Unit of time, 3600 seconds

concepts.label: Day
concepts.definition: Unit of time, 86400 seconds, one Earth rotation

concepts.label: Year
concepts.definition: Unit of time, ~365.25 days, one Earth orbit around Sun

concepts.label: Atomic Mass Unit
concepts.definition: Unit of mass, 1 u = 1/12 mass of carbon-12, ~1.661×10⁻²⁷ kg

concepts.label: Bohr Radius
concepts.definition: Most probable distance of electron from nucleus in hydrogen ground state ~0.529 Å

concepts.label: Hubble Time
concepts.definition: Reciprocal of Hubble constant, approximate age of universe ~14 Gyr

concepts.label: Unit of Measurement
concepts.definition: Standard quantity used to express physical quantities

concepts.label: SI Units
concepts.definition: International System of Units, modern metric system, seven base units

concepts.label: CGS Units
concepts.definition: Centimeter-gram-second system of units, older metric system

concepts.label: Speed of Light
concepts.definition: Fundamental constant c = 299792458 m/s exactly, maximum speed in universe

concepts.label: Gravitational Constant
concepts.definition: G = 6.674×10⁻¹¹ N⋅m²/kg², strength of gravitational interaction

concepts.label: Planck Constant
concepts.definition: h = 6.626×10⁻³⁴ J⋅s, fundamental quantum of action

concepts.label: Boltzmann Constant
concepts.definition: k = 1.381×10⁻²³ J/K, relates temperature to energy

concepts.label: Avogadro Constant
concepts.definition: Nₐ = 6.022×10²³ mol⁻¹, number of entities in one mole

concepts.label: Elementary Charge
concepts.definition: e = 1.602×10⁻¹⁹ C, charge of proton

concepts.label: Vacuum Permittivity
concepts.definition: ε₀ = 8.854×10⁻¹² F/m, electric constant

concepts.label: Vacuum Permeability
concepts.definition: μ₀ = 4π×10⁻⁷ H/m, magnetic constant

concepts.label: Fine-Structure Constant
concepts.definition: α ≈ 1/137.036, dimensionless coupling constant of electromagnetism

concepts.label: Rydberg Constant
concepts.definition: R∞ = 1.097×10⁷ m⁻¹, relates to hydrogen spectral lines

concepts.label: Gas Constant
concepts.definition: R = 8.314 J/mol/K, relates to ideal gas law

concepts.label: Neutron Mass
concepts.definition: mₙ = 1.675×10⁻²⁷ kg, slightly more than proton

concepts.label: Physical Constant
concepts.definition: Fundamental fixed value in physics that does not change

concepts.label: Nebula
concepts.definition: Cloud of gas and dust in space, can be star-forming or remnant

concepts.label: Planetary Nebula
concepts.definition: Glowing shell of gas expelled by dying low-mass star

concepts.label: Supernova Remnant
concepts.definition: Expanding shell of gas from supernova explosion

concepts.label: Orion Nebula
concepts.definition: Nearest massive star-forming region M42, visible to naked eye in Orion

concepts.label: Crab Nebula
concepts.definition: Supernova remnant M1 in Taurus from 1054 CE supernova, contains pulsar

concepts.label: Eagle Nebula
concepts.definition: Star-forming region M16, Pillars of Creation photographed by Hubble

concepts.label: Helix Nebula
concepts.definition: Nearest planetary nebula NGC 7293, Eye of God

concepts.label: Ring Nebula
concepts.definition: Planetary nebula M57 in Lyra

concepts.label: Asteroid Belt
concepts.definition: Region between Mars and Jupiter containing rocky bodies

concepts.label: Kuiper Belt
concepts.definition: Region beyond Neptune containing icy bodies including Pluto

concepts.label: Oort Cloud
concepts.definition: Hypothetical spherical shell of icy objects surrounding Solar System at ~50000-100000 AU

concepts.label: Comet
concepts.definition: Icy body that develops coma and tail when approaching Sun

concepts.label: Asteroid
concepts.definition: Rocky body orbiting Sun, smaller than planet, mostly in asteroid belt

concepts.label: Meteor
concepts.definition: Streak of light from space debris burning in Earth atmosphere

concepts.label: Meteorite
concepts.definition: Space rock that survives passage through atmosphere and reaches ground

concepts.label: Exoplanet
concepts.definition: Planet orbiting star other than the Sun, thousands discovered

concepts.label: Hot Jupiter
concepts.definition: Gas giant exoplanet orbiting very close to its star

concepts.label: Habitable Zone
concepts.definition: Region around star where liquid water could exist on planet surface

concepts.label: Pulsar Wind Nebula
concepts.definition: Nebula powered by pulsar relativistic wind

concepts.label: Accretion Disk
concepts.definition: Disk of material spiraling into massive object like black hole

concepts.label: Gamma-Ray Burst
concepts.definition: Most energetic events in universe, brief intense gamma-ray flashes

concepts.label: Magnetosphere
concepts.definition: Region around planet dominated by its magnetic field

concepts.label: Van Allen Radiation Belts
concepts.definition: Zones of charged particles trapped by Earth magnetic field

concepts.label: Aurora
concepts.definition: Light display from charged particles exciting atmospheric gases at magnetic poles

concepts.label: Solar Wind
concepts.definition: Stream of charged particles ejected from Sun upper atmosphere

concepts.label: Solar Flare
concepts.definition: Sudden flash of increased brightness on Sun from magnetic reconnection

concepts.label: Coronal Mass Ejection
concepts.definition: Large expulsion of plasma and magnetic field from solar corona

concepts.label: Sunspot
concepts.definition: Temporary dark area on Sun surface caused by magnetic flux concentration

concepts.label: Tidal Locking
concepts.definition: Rotation period equals orbital period so same face always toward partner

concepts.label: Lagrange Points
concepts.definition: Five equilibrium points in two-body gravitational system

concepts.label: Higgs Mechanism
concepts.definition: Process by which particles acquire mass through interaction with Higgs field

concepts.label: Quantum Mechanics
concepts.definition: Fundamental theory of physics describing nature at atomic and subatomic scales

concepts.label: Low Earth Orbit
concepts.definition: Orbit around Earth at altitude 160-2000 km

concepts.label: LIGO
concepts.definition: Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory, detected gravitational waves 2015
